title: Government-Paid Adoption Benefit (GPAB)
text:
Government-Paid Adoption Benefit (GPAB) - SupportGoWhere
Government-Paid Adoption Benefit (GPAB)
Share link
Provides up to 12 weeks of Government-paid income for eligible adoptive working mothers who do not qualify for Adoption Leave for Mothers (AL).
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to 8 weeks of Government-paid income for 1st and 2nd child, capped at $20,000
Up to 12 weeks of Government-paid income for 3rd and subsequent child, capped at $30,000
For adoptive mothers who do not qualify for AL due to their employment arrangements
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to 8 weeks of Government-paid income for the first and second child, capped at $20,000.

Up to 12 weeks of Government-paid income for the third and subsequent child, capped at $30,000.

Who is this for?

Adopting a child in accordance with any Singapore law relating to the adopting of children

You are not the natural mother of the child

Child is below 12 months old on Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date

FIA date on or after 1 January 2021

If child is not a Singapore Citizen on FIA date, a Dependent’s Pass has been issued and either you or your husband has been a Singapore Citizen since the Dependent’s Pass issue date

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before FIA date

You do not qualify for the AL scheme Adoption Leave (AL) except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment

How to apply?

Record employment contract dates or trade/business periods from the 12 months before your Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date.

Submit your claim online within 15 months of your child's birth.

An officer will contact you post-submission.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222 
(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
